title_en: Independent factual verification of the deep-research baseline

findings.statement: No KH-INS ID is explicitly adopted on canonical Інструкції; all 22 remain recommendations even where static code aligns.
P-001 through P-004 lack traceable REQ-IDs, so REQ-0004's owner-request gate is unsatisfied; only P-005 links to REQ-0002.
The proposed Telegram action set is partial and nonexclusive in static code.
Privacy claims lack complete primary proof; KH-PRV-001 is report-only and one tracking match remains unresolved.
No E3-E5 proof was produced; Versie 1 acceptance remains incomplete.

limitations: No tests were run, as required.
No Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, OAuth, deployment, protected store, secret value, browser, runtime, staging, production, or network state was accessed.
Evidence was limited to immutable local Git objects and static inspection; local origin refs were not network-refreshed.
Knowledge-hub prose and report extractions were treated only as claims under test.
File presence and lexical markers do not prove behavior; the single tracking match was not contextually resolved during the one allowed implementation read.
No contradiction status was assigned because the proven gaps are absent adoption, incomplete request traceability, and missing behavioral proof rather than proven opposite behavior.

findings.summary_uk: Канонічний Gmail/Apps Script/Telegram продукт суперечить report-derived цільовому Mailcow/Stalwart core, запропонованому src-tree та Pub/Sub/Cloud Run event flow.
findings.summary_en: The canonical Gmail/Apps Script/Telegram product contradicts the report-derived Mailcow/Stalwart core, proposed src tree, and Pub/Sub/Cloud Run event flow.

evidence.method: Immutable file inspection reconciled with the Ukrainian twin.
evidence.result: The current mission is a safe Gmail client in Telegram for one owner and explicitly connected accounts; the active Versie branch and current roadmap/issues are authoritative.

evidence.method: Exact immutable manifest inspection.
evidence.result: The project enables Gmail v1 and requests gmail.modify, drive.readonly, script.external_request, and script.scriptapp; the web app executes as the deployer and allows anonymous ingress that application code must authenticate.

evidence.method: Full immutable git ls-tree inventory plus targeted static inspection.
evidence.result: The repository is Apps Script, static callback, documentation, tests, and release tooling; no Mailcow, Stalwart, Dovecot, Postfix, Redis, PostgreSQL, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run, or Microsoft Graph implementation exists.

evidence.method: Immutable architecture-contract inspection, corroborated where possible by current code.
evidence.result: A Gmail/Telegram identity-to-zone-to-role-to-connection contract and a future partitioned storage path are documented; Mailcow, Stalwart, and Microsoft Graph are not the current implementation.

findings.summary_uk: CI перевіряє лише документацію та knowledge hub; CodeQL, Dependabot config, deploy/dry-run, staging smoke і security lane відсутні, а server-side secret scanning не підтверджено.
findings.summary_en: CI validates only documentation and the knowledge hub; CodeQL, Dependabot configuration, deploy/dry-run, staging smoke, and a security lane are absent, while server-side secret scanning is unproven.

evidence.method: Exact workflow inspection plus immutable .github tree inventory.
evidence.result: This workflow checks bilingual documentation only with read-only contents permission.

evidence.method: Exact workflow inspection plus immutable .github tree inventory.
evidence.result: This workflow validates bilingual knowledge-hub hygiene only; no CodeQL, Dependabot configuration, deploy workflow, staging smoke lane, or separate security lane exists at the commit.

evidence.method: Exact ignore-policy inspection.
evidence.result: Credential-like files, tokens, keys, logs, caches, and local artifacts are excluded by pattern; this does not prove GitHub server-side secret scanning.

findings.summary_uk: Network/runtime traces і повний acceptance package не можуть бути підтверджені Git-only перевіркою; Versie 1 не promoted/tagged і не має release branch.
findings.summary_en: Network/runtime traces and the complete acceptance package cannot be established by Git-only verification; Versie 1 is not promoted, tagged, or represented by a release branch.

evidence.method: Immutable release-article inspection; prose was treated as a claim unless corroborated by code or Git metadata.
evidence.result: Versie 1 is explicitly in progress and not promoted, tagged, or represented by a release branch; production acceptance remains incomplete.

evidence.method: Local git show-ref, tag, branch, merge-base, and divergence inspection at the assigned commit.
evidence.result: The active local and origin Versie branch matched the assigned commit; no tag or release/versie-001 ref existed, and main was on a divergent publication lineage.

evidence.method: Immutable bilingual roadmap inspection.
evidence.result: Versie 1 is the only active version; local fixes, manual OAuth acceptance, immutable staging, promotion, cleanup, tag, and release branch are distinct gates.

findings.summary_uk: Більшість state/token paths мають locks, але Gmail refresh function не має локального lock; без concurrency test або trace ризик race залишається частково підтвердженим.
findings.summary_en: Most state/token paths use locks, but the Gmail refresh function has no local lock; without a concurrency test or trace, the race risk remains partially substantiated.

evidence.method: Single-pass static OAuth, identity, state, and lock inspection with sensitive literals redacted.
evidence.result: Found Telegram-user/zone/connection authorization, one-use OAuth state, separate protected token records, profile avatar capture, account-scoped registries, and numerous locks; the Gmail access-token refresh function has no lock local to that function.

evidence.method: Single-pass static function/API/pattern inspection with sensitive literals redacted.
evidence.result: Found account-scoped RPC, Focus rules, four-way attention triage, backlog rescue, co-processing, functional metrics, evidence-grounded handoff, sanitizers, URL/DNS checks, operation journals, scheduled send, sessions, and lock-protected state paths.

evidence.method: Static test-name and relevant contract inspection only; tests were not executed.
evidence.result: Definitions cover replay-resistant sessions, token/state locks, account isolation, Focus, triage, energy/reminders, co-processing, backlog rescue, content-free metrics, URL safety, idempotent send, and scheduled send.

findings.summary_uk: Release/callback artifacts містять environment-specific identifiers, а release helper також machine-local recovery path; значення вилучено. Прямого secret logging не знайдено, але повний historical/dataflow scan не виконувався.
findings.summary_en: Release/callback artifacts contain environment-specific identifiers, and the release helper also contains a machine-local recovery path; values are redacted. No direct secret logging was found, but a full historical/dataflow scan was not performed.

evidence.method: Static inspection with all values redacted.
evidence.result: The public helper contains environment-specific project/deployment identifiers and a machine-local recovery-path literal; no value is reproduced in this report.

evidence.method: Exact immutable callback-relay inspection; environment-specific endpoint value omitted.
evidence.result: The relay clears the query from browser history, validates bounded state and code, forwards only action/state/code, and fails closed on malformed input.

evidence.method: Single-pass static function/API/pattern inspection with sensitive literals redacted.
evidence.result: Found owner/same-email notification deduplication, bounded delivery ledgers, polling and continuation state, Script/User locks, one-use launch nonces, webhook-key validation, reminders, Gmail History synchronization, and Telegram callbacks.

evidence.method: Single-pass static function/API/pattern inspection with sensitive literals redacted.
evidence.result: Found account-scoped RPC, Focus rules, four-way attention triage, backlog rescue, co-processing, functional metrics, evidence-grounded handoff, sanitizers, URL/DNS checks, operation journals, scheduled send, sessions, and lock-protected state paths.

findings.summary_uk: Загальні human-factor, AI-risk і зовнішні mail-stack твердження не верифікуються Git evidence цього продукту та залишаються unverified.
findings.summary_en: General human-factor, AI-risk, and external mail-stack claims cannot be verified from this product's Git evidence and remain unverified.

evidence.method: The English and Ukrainian source pages were read once and reconciled by canonical ID.
evidence.result: This records the assigned report-derived claim and is not independent evidence of implementation or runtime behavior.

evidence.method: Immutable file inspection reconciled with the Ukrainian twin.
evidence.result: The current mission is a safe Gmail client in Telegram for one owner and explicitly connected accounts; the active Versie branch and current roadmap/issues are authoritative.

findings.summary_uk: Твердження про недоступний repository audit застаріло: commit доступний, code/tests/history індексуються, а audit artifacts присутні; runtime audit усе ще не доведено.
findings.summary_en: The unavailable-repository-audit claim is stale: the commit is accessible, code/tests/history are indexable, and audit artifacts exist; a runtime audit remains unproven.

evidence.method: Immutable canonical main-branch governance inspection.
evidence.result: The hub is explicitly report-derived baseline, not production-state evidence; Requests, Instructions, Permissions, and the active Versie are separate canonical areas.

evidence.method: Immutable tree and history inspection only; audit prose was not accepted as proof by repetition.
evidence.result: Multiple audit artifacts exist, disproving repository unavailability but not proving a complete runtime audit.

evidence.method: Single-pass static function/API/pattern inspection with sensitive literals redacted.
evidence.result: Found owner/same-email notification deduplication, bounded delivery ledgers, polling and continuation state, Script/User locks, one-use launch nonces, webhook-key validation, reminders, Gmail History synchronization, and Telegram callbacks.

limitations: No tests were executed by instruction; static test definitions are not E3 evidence.
No Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, OAuth browser flow, staging, production, protected store, deployment, or external service was accessed.
No web browsing or external primary-source verification was performed; general human-factor and external mail-stack claims remain E0 where Git cannot prove them.
Remote-tracking refs were inspected from the local repository without a network fetch; conclusions are commit-bound to the assigned immutable hash and locally available canonical refs.
File presence proves presence only. Static implementation inspection is capped at E2 and does not prove runtime behavior.
Environment-specific identifiers, credential values, private message data, and machine-local paths are omitted or redacted.

findings.summary: The pinned repository does not use the claimed apps-script-oauth2 dependency. It implements custom external-provider OAuth handlers with Properties/Lock practices and declares no Apps Script OAuth2 library.
The active manifest declares a web app and Gmail API advanced service but no Gmail add-on configuration.
No mail-server stack, Node mail package, Redis, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run, PGP/S-MIME, PWA push, native-mobile, or desktop-framework dependency/configuration is declared in the pinned tree.
Executive-function, cognitive-load, low-friction, and accessibility outcomes cannot be proven statically; each blocked claim names the exact safe acceptance or standards evidence required.
The source implements deterministic heuristics and labels some output as automated AI analysis, but no statistical-model or external AI dependency is declared.
verified=13; partial=26; contradicted=1; unverified=12; blocked=4; recommendation=16

limitations: Verification was read-only at immutable commit 2b3b9e2f678f9fa0c787247f92d7827f81e95c9a.
Only the bilingual PRODUCT.md and DEPENDENCIES.md knowledge-hub pages supplied assigned claims; report prose was not treated as evidence.
No tests were run, so test_runs is empty and no claim exceeds E2.
No Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, OAuth browser flow, protected credential store, staging/runtime, deployment, or production system was accessed.
No external browsing was performed; current vendor capabilities, versions, licenses, standards, policies, and limits remain unverified unless repository implementation alone was the claim.
Local canonical branch refs and ancestry were inspected without fetching; branch-tip freshness beyond the local repository was not established.
File presence and release scripts do not prove deployment, runtime activation, or acceptance.

test_runs.reason: The assigned stream explicitly prohibited running tests. Test files were inspected statically only, so no claim received E3.

findings.summary: The tracked backend creates a recurring time-based checkNewMail_ trigger; watch/history checkpoint ingestion is not the active tracked implementation.

Apps Script is the full authenticated mailbox backend and owns polling, notifications, Telegram transport, sessions, drafts, attachments, and Gmail operations rather than acting only as thin ingress/control plane.
The pinned repository and canonical Instructions branch designate Versie 1 as the active working line; v45 remains lineage, not the current baseline designation.
The inspected implementation is Gmail/Apps Script/Telegram and contains no tracked Mailcow, Stalwart, Postfix/Dovecot, Rspamd/SpamAssassin, or DAV-stack implementation.
The tracked Mini App is explicitly a full Gmail mail client, not only a dashboard/control surface, although Telegram cards are bounded and Gmail links remain available.
Behavioral generalizations, concrete provenance markers, and upstream licensing/maintenance histories lack admissible primary evidence in the inspected project Git record.

limitations: Read-only verification was limited to the pinned repository commit, immutable ancestor commits, release and architecture artifacts, static implementation and test-contract inspection, and canonical governance branch records.
No tests were run; no claim is graded E3.
No Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script deployment, OAuth flow, protected store, credential, or runtime state was accessed; no claim is graded E4 or E5.
Knowledge-hub pages and prior report prose were treated as claims and provenance only, never as corroboration by repetition.
File presence and static code establish tracked implementation only; they do not prove current deployment or runtime behavior.
Negative repository searches do not rule out external, private, deleted, or untracked artifacts.

test_runs.working_directory: repository root at the assigned immutable commit
test_runs.safety_review: Entrypoints were inspected first. Selected files use local Node assertions, filesystem/source fixtures, VM mocks, and one PowerShell syntax-parse subprocess. No OAuth, Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, production, network, or protected-store access was performed.
test_runs.verification_scope: local deterministic implementation tests only; not runtime acceptance

findings.summary: The checked project is a web app, not the claimed Gmail contextual add-on: the manifest has no addOns/contextualTriggers and the implementation has no CardService.
v45 is an immutable ancestor, but it is not an unchanged active tree; core UI/routing files changed after v45 and Versie 1 still has uncompleted release gates.
The repository supports web-app, Gmail History, state/lock, and partial clasp mechanics, but contains no add-on, Pub/Sub/Cloud Run, CodeQL, or Dependabot configuration.
External scientific, product, standards, regulatory, library, documentation, and indexing assertions have no independent primary evidence in the assigned local Git scope.
Status totals: {"unverified":10,"partial":18,"contradicted":1,"blocked":1}. TR-001 passed 399/399 local deterministic tests, which does not constitute E4/E5 runtime or production acceptance.

limitations: Verification was restricted to the assigned local repository and immutable Git objects; no external browsing or network evidence was used.
No Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, OAuth, staging, production, deployment, runtime, or protected credential store was accessed.
E3 results are local automated tests with mocks/static fixtures and one PowerShell syntax parse; they cannot establish runtime behavior.
Release and knowledge-hub prose were treated as claims/status artifacts, not proof by repetition; only implementation, immutable hashes/history, manifests, and executed tests raised evidence grades.
No claim reached E4 or E5. Runtime/production assertions require exact safe read-only deployment/version readback and acceptance evidence in a separately authorized stream.
No OAuth, Gmail, Telegram, Apps Script, network, staging, or production action was executed for VR-001.
E1 and E2 findings prove repository history or implementation presence only, not runtime behavior.
Absence findings are scoped to tracked repository artifacts at the target commit and named governance commits.
The source baseline is report-derived; VR-001 assigns claim-level status rather than blanket trust.
